title: Who Fired the Shot
wikitext:
[]: Who Fired the Shot?             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Sep 30, 2011 3:58 am You know, it's always a great thing to be able to go back through Eva and to find new things that you didn't really notice or focus on before. Whether great things that can change the entire dynamic of perspective of Eva, or small things that are just kind of neat. I was just watching EoE, really trying to treat it as if it were my first time again, and something occurred to me during the JSSDF's invasion of Nerv. At one point, Maya has a breakdown and screams "I shot at targets, not human beings!" Immediately after, a shot flies by her head. Despite being a shot from off-screen, we can easily determine where the shot came from: some random JSSDF soldier. Or can we? The Bridge Bunny platform in Central Dogma is the highest above all other levels, in exception to where Gendo and Fuyutsuki usually watch from. If we take a look at the shape their platform's walls, we see that they're at a position in which a shot from anywhere angled below them will not have a high chance in hitting anybody inside. It's safe to assume that they're highest in contrast to the soldiers, otherwise, if they were attacking from above, the Bridge Bunnies would not be surviving so efficiently. It's quite clear that the Bridge Bunnies have the higher ground here against the soldiers We also ought to remember Maya is underneath a desk. As clearly shown here: So, if Maya is hiding underneath a desk, and they're pretty much the highest tier in the command center, where the hell did that shot come from? If it couldn't be any soldier from beyond their platform, then it must have come from some other source. The shot can neither be from Maya nor Aoba, as they clearly flinch in fear from the shot. Who else is left on the platform, but Hyuga ? No, seriously. Where'd this shot come from?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Sep 30, 2011 6:59 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Sep 30, 2011 7:23 am Hyuga must be rusty, I remember maya saying that they only have basic weapons training,ether that or hyuga must be a really bad shot.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Fri Sep 30, 2011 11:54 am Hearing Hyuga saying: "Sorry, gun's safe was unlocked" right in that moment would have been hilarious.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Sep 30, 2011 4:23 pm Ah, I always knew he was evil. Probably shot Kaji too, to have free reign with Misato...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 30, 2011 4:26 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Fanwankery for the win: It was a ricochet off of the higher tier (Gendo and Fuyu's) from a shot fired from below.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Sep 30, 2011 4:59 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: This is what I assumed as well.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Sep 30, 2011 5:01 pm I just watched the scene a few times, it looks like we don't actually see a bullet right? It's a very fast scene, but I don't see any bullet flying past them, even on slow mo. I just saw dust fly, like something hit the wall. So, based on that, here's some thoughts. The bullet hit the outside wall, JSSDF fired it. The "Dust" was from the impact vibrations. It may have gone through the wall, but I doubt it because if that texture was that thin/weak they wouldn't have survived very long.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Fri Sep 30, 2011 5:26 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Fanwankery for the win: It was a ricochet off of the higher tier (Gendo and Fuyu's) from a shot fired from below. Also, very good catch. Fun post too. "So, if Mari is hiding underneath a desk" ... What's Mari got to do with it? Yeah I know, typo.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Fri Sep 30, 2011 5:34 pm CJD wrote: The bullet hit the outside wall, JSSDF fired it. The "Dust" was from the impact vibrations. It may have gone through the wall, but I doubt it because if that texture was that thin/weak they wouldn't have survived very long.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Sep 30, 2011 5:49 pm John wrote: I don't think so, since there's that weird bullet "whistle" which is only heard when the bullet passes really close to you, then it's still passing, but not stopping. If the 5.1 audio channel it's true to Anno's expectations, and my Sennheisser headphones aren't lying to me, it would be like this: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Sep 30, 2011 6:10 pm Oh christ! First it was just funny and now you are all going batshit fanwank crazy about it. I honestly can't believe that every little effing detail warrants a discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Sep 30, 2011 6:22 pm die-yng wrote: Oh christ! First it was just funny and now you are all going batshit fanwank crazy about it. I honestly can't believe that every little effing detail warrants a discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Fri Sep 30, 2011 6:25 pm At first I also found the whole thread funny but after watching that scene again, I couldn't believe how much detail they put on that insignificant sound effect. (

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Fri Sep 30, 2011 8:32 pm xanderkh wrote: You realize this is Evangelion we're talking about, right?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Oct 01, 2011 2:51 pm Pen-Pen did it. Motherfucker's been a SEELE spy this whole time.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 01, 2011 6:45 pm Kendrix wrote: either that or just an oversight/Rule of Drama. It's more dramatic to have the shot go above them to drive home the point of how hopelessly surrounded they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Oct 01, 2011 8:34 pm Either it's a ricochet or Hyuga is hilariously incompetent with weapons. I'm rather partial to the latter.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sun Oct 02, 2011 1:17 am IMHO: The Jietai boys had more training and experience than those up on the bridge, so even if the latter had the upper ground, nevertheless besides being inexperienced in marksmanship, they're at the mercy of military snipers who'll pick them off the minute they stand up and try to shoot with their heads peeking, or so easy for a soldier to fire off a M203 grenade round or a mortar round into any of the upper bridges.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Oct 02, 2011 6:29 am John wrote: Hearing Hyuga saying: "Sorry, gun's safe was unlocked" right in that moment would have been hilarious.
